candidate_sources_rejected: heat intolerance used alone to diagnose hyperthyroidism
heat illness guidance applied to chronic intolerance without exposure
automatic thyroid testing, medicine, dose, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Acute environmental heat illness is a different presentation.
No investigation or treatment is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: No legacy item in gp-heat-intolerance remains source-supported after conservative correction; item-specific population applicability was not independently established without new research.
setting_applicability: No legacy item in gp-heat-intolerance remains source-supported after conservative correction; transfer from the recorded source setting to the exact workflow item was not independently established.
UAE_applicability: insufficient_evidence for every removed gp-heat-intolerance legacy-item mapping; UAE applicability requires independent item-level review before any mapping is reinstated.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact suspected-thyroid section supports clinician review of persistent symptoms, mood or anxiety, medicine, biotin, illness, and thyroid-test context without treating heat intolerance alone as diagnostic.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact information and warning section supports clinician-assessed examination, systemic concern, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up without diagnosing hyperthyroidism.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

unresolved_source_gaps: Environment, measured temperature, sweating, weight, pulse, pregnancy, menopause, medicines, thyroid signs, test values, cause, diagnosis, treatment, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
